description_long: This object consists of two large, irregularly shaped white garbage bags pressed together, with visible creases and folds in the plastic material. The bags appear to be filled to capacity, creating bulging, rounded forms with soft, uneven surfaces. Subtle blue and gray shadows accentuate the contours, suggesting the bags are made of thin, semi-opaque plastic. There are no visible printed labels, logos, or text on the exterior, and the contents inside are not discernible. The bags are shown resting on the ground, with no visible ties or closures at the top. The overall appearance is typical of garbage bags left out for collection in an urban environment.
description: Two bulging white plastic garbage bags, pressed together and resting on the ground, with visible creases and no printed markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the overlapping, bulging forms of both garbage bags with prominent creases and soft shadows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and irregular profile of the bags, with one bag partially obscuring the other.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the broad, rounded surface of the bags and the subtle color variations in the plastic.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the bags' fullness and the way they press against each other, with more visible creasing.
attribute_fullness_or_bulging: bulging and fully filled, with taut surfaces and pronounced creases
